name: MAIA Biotechnology, Inc.
business: MAIA Biotechnology, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing targeted immunotherapies for cancer. Its lead asset, ateganosine (THIO), is an investigational dual mechanism of action drug candidate incorporating telomere targeting and immunogenicity, currently in clinical trials for non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). The company plans to seek accelerated approval for ateganosine in the United States in 2026.
sellsTo: patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer
third-line NSCLC patients resistant to checkpoint inhibitors and chemotherapy

exposures.sentence: MAIA Biotechnology, Inc. (MAIA, the Company, we, or us) is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing targeted immunotherapies for cancer.

catalysts.event: FDA granted Fast Track designation for ateganosine for NSCLC
catalysts.sentence: On July 28, 2025, we announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Fast Track designation for ateganosine (THIO, 6-thio-dG or 6-thio2 -deoxyguanosine) for the treatment of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC).

catalysts.event: First patient dosed in THIO-104 Phase 3 pivotal trial
catalysts.sentence: On December 11, 2025, we announced that the first patient has been dosed in THIO-104 Phase 3 pivotal trial evaluating the efficacy of ateganosine administered in sequence with a checkpoint inhibitor (CPI) as a third-line treatment for advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC).

catalysts.event: NIH awarded $2.3 million grant for THIO-101 expansion
catalysts.sentence: On September 24, 2025, we announced that the National Institutes of Health (NIH) has awarded us a $2.3 million grant for the expansion of its THIO-101 Phase 2 clinical trial evaluating ateganosine as a third-line treatment for patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC).

catalysts.event: Plan to seek accelerated approval filing for ateganosine in the US in 2026
catalysts.sentence: Based on the clinical data generated by our THIO-101 trial, we plan to seek filing for an accelerated approval of ateganosine in the United States for the treatment of patients with advanced NSCLC in 2026, but even if granted, accelerated approval status does not guarantee an accelerated review or marketing approval by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

catalysts.event: Plan to initiate Phase 2 trials in HCC, CRC, and SCLC in 2026
catalysts.sentence: Phase 2 clinical trials in HCC, CRC and SCLC are planned to be initiated in 2026, evaluating treatment with ateganosine administered in sequence with BeOne's immune checkpoint inhibitor, tislelizumab.

catalysts.event: Plan to start Phase 1 trials for second-generation molecules
catalysts.sentence: The targeted milestones include: ... clinical development of second-generation molecules planned to start in Phase 1 trials, with additional small molecules fully developed in-house with better expected efficacy compared to ateganosine.

catalysts.event: Interim efficacy measures from Phase 3 study anticipated in 2026
catalysts.sentence: The targeted milestones include: initial measures of efficacy from Phase 3 study, with interim disease control rates (DCR), overall response rates (ORR) and progression free survival (PFS) analysis of ateganosine compared to the control arm will support regulatory discussions.

tamClaims.claim: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer had an incidence of 2.1 million in 2022, 5-year prevalence of 2.7 million, mortality of 1.5 million, annual sales of $30.8 billion in 2025 projected to $48.9 billion in 2029.
tamClaims.sentence: Tumor Type Incidence 2022 (M) 5-Year Prevalence 2022 (M) Mortality 2022 (M) Annual Sales 2025 ($B) Annual Sales 2029 ($B) (projected) Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer 2.1 2.7 1.5 30.8 48.9

tamClaims.claim: Total sales of checkpoint inhibitor franchises in 2024 were $51.8 billion.
